name: SmplTrek
use: Multi-track sampling, scene-based arrangement, drum kits, looper recording
overview: Project-based 15-pad sampler/groovebox. A project = multiple scenes; each scene = 10 sequencer tracks + 3 audio tracks. Play full project (scenes advance) or launch individual scenes as loops for live improv. 5 control knobs + directional pad.
workflow: Power on → Home Screen auto-opens; press project button to load/browse projects
Press <kbd>STEP</kbd> buttons to launch and loop individual scenes (each loops until you pick another)
Press <kbd>PLAY</kbd> to run the full project — scenes advance automatically in order
Hold a track pad → select REC SRC → confirm → begins quick sampling directly to that track
Resample: <kbd>Func</kbd> + <kbd>Rec</kbd> → select source track → <kbd>Rec</kbd> to capture
Loop track record: press <kbd>Rec</kbd> on the Edit screen of a LOOP track → plays back and re-records simultaneously
Drum kit: press <kbd>DRUM</kbd> track button → select kit in pool → 15 pads = individual drum sounds
Edit sample: press <kbd>Edit</kbd> → use direction buttons + VALUE knob to set chop points, trim, pitch, stretch
Press <kbd>Mixer</kbd> button → use knobs A–D to set levels, pan, and send FX per track
Export: <kbd>Mixer</kbd> → scroll to Mixdown or Stem → <kbd>OK</kbd> to bounce to file

shortcuts.action: Play full project (scenes advance in order, no loop) ★
Launch specific scene as a loop ★
Quick sampling — record directly to that track ★
Open input settings (mic, line L/R, guitar, etc.)
Open track editor (chop, trim, normalize, stretch)

trackTypes: Drum Track — step sequencer for drum kits (max 4/project)
MIDI Track — piano-roll for external MIDI gear (max 10/project)
tips: Scene = loops; Project playback = advances scenes — use scenes for live improv, project for final arrangement
Clip shorter than scene length will loop; clip longer than scene will be cut off at scene boundary
Quick sampling: hold a pad on the Home Screen — fastest way to capture an idea without menu navigation
Resampling (Func+Rec): capture the SmplTrek's own output — good for bouncing a live improv to a single clip
